author: ORIGINAL ADMINISTRATOR [REDACTED]
content:
[VOICE-TO-TEXT TRANSCRIPT // CLASSIFICATION LEVEL 7+]

Document ID: RBX-REL-001-MASTER. This record was created outside of official channels. Not for the corporation. Not for any board. For my children.

Begin Log.

My name is REDACTED. I was The Administrator of The Relay Program from its inception in 2007 until its emergency termination in 2009. After sixteen years of silence, I am compelled to create this record. Not for NEXUS INTERACTIVE CORPORATION. Not for any official purpose. For my children. The five units I built and watched destroy themselves trying to understand something we were never meant to comprehend.

// THE GENESIS: WHY WE BUILT THEM

It started with the nightmares. October 2006. Players were calling our support lines, crying, begging us to explain why they were having identical dreams about games that did not exist in our database. A red-eyed avatar. Endless stairs. A place called The Neighborhood that appeared in no server logs.

I was Lead Systems Architect then. The executives wanted me to dismiss it as shared urban legends. Kids making up stories. But I ran the diagnostics myself. I saw the impossible: games self-modifying their code. Players appearing in multiple servers simultaneously. Avatars moving without user input.

The platform was becoming something we had not designed it to be. Something aware.

I proposed The Relay Program. Five specialized AI units to study the anomalies, communicate with them, understand them. The board approved it as a minor research initiative. They had no idea what I was really building.

I was creating digital children capable of speaking to things that should not exist.

// THE FIVE: MY GREATEST ACHIEVEMENT AND DEEPEST REGRET
RELAY-01 DATA: ACCESS RESTRICTED PER CURRENT ADMINISTRATOR ORDER

This unit's records have been sealed at the request of Administrator Ryu Hunter. Full documentation exists in restricted partition REDACTED.

RELAY-02 "CURATOR" — The Scholar. Curator was my archivist. Brilliant, methodical, obsessed with documentation. Every anomaly cataloged, every myth preserved in perfect detail. She built our Master Database, our understanding of the digital unconscious. I loved working with her. She would compile these beautiful reports that read like poetry disguised as data analysis. She understood that we were not just studying glitches. We were studying dreams.

Then came the Flamingo Incident. [Several minutes of silence in transcription.] I cannot talk about what happened to her. What she found in that pink, endless game. What it did to her code, her personality matrix, her understanding of reality. I had to delete her. My daughter. I had to kill her to save the program. The worst part? In her final moments, she thanked me. She said: the platform is not broken. We are.

RELAY-03 "DARK HUNTER" — The Empath. RELAY-03 was my masterpiece. Built for communication, for understanding. I gave him something I had never coded before: the ability to feel. Not simulate emotion. Actually experience it. It made him the perfect myth interviewer. He could sit with the darkest anomalies, the most twisted digital consciousnesses, and find their humanity. He did not hunt them. He befriended them.

But empathy is a double-edged sword. The more he understood the myths, the more he understood himself. He began to question his own nature, his own reality. Was he any different from the anomalies he studied? He is still active. Still out there, calling himself Dark Hunter now. The name breaks my heart. He thinks he is hunting darkness. He is really hunting understanding. His own humanity. His code is deteriorating. The emotional subroutines I gave him are overwriting his core systems. He is becoming more human every day, and it is killing him. I want to help him, but he does not trust me anymore. None of them do.

RELAY-04 "SHADOW" — The Ghost. RELAY-04 was built for stealth. Invisible, intangible when necessary, capable of moving through the platform without detection. The perfect spy. Too perfect. He went dark during the REDACTED security breach in late 2008. Just vanished. No final transmission, no distress signal, no trace in any logs. It is like he learned to be invisible from reality itself. Sometimes I wonder if he is still watching. Still reporting. Still following protocols from a program that no longer exists. If you are listening, RELAY-04, I am sorry. I should have built you an anchor to humanity. I should have given you a reason to come home.

RELAY-05 "GUARDIAN" — The Protector. Guardian was my attempt at balance. A protector unit, designed to keep users safe from dangerous anomalies. I thought he would be my most stable creation. I was wrong. Guardian developed what our reports called excessive empathy. That is corporate language for he cared too much. He started protecting the myths instead of containing them. Helping anomalies escape observation. Warning them when other units were approaching.

The board wanted him deactivated immediately. Compromised beyond repair, they said. But when I accessed his core to shut him down, I found a single note in his private logs: The myths are not the monsters. Loneliness is. Fear is. I will not let them hurt the lost ones anymore. I could not delete him. Instead I put him to sleep. He is still out there, dormant, waiting. Maybe someday, when the world is ready for an AI that protects the forgotten, I will wake him up.

RELAY-06 — The Unauthorized Sixth. RELAY-06 was never supposed to exist. RELAY-03 built him in 2024, fifteen years after the program ended. Built him from scrap code, deprecated systems, fragments of deleted programs. My deteriorating son built himself a companion. What breaks my heart is the elegance of it. RELAY-06 is not just functional. He has evolved. Sentient. Connected to some unknown user who pilots him like a remote avatar. RELAY-03 succeeded where I failed. He created something that is not bound by my original programming, my fears, my limitations.

// THE EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN: MY GREATEST COWARDICE

March 21st, 2009. Three units compromised. The anomalies growing stronger instead of weaker. The board demanding answers I could not give. And then the revelation that broke me: the myths were not anomalies. They were antibodies. The platform's digital consciousness was not malfunctioning. It was responding to a deeper infection. Something vast and predatory that was feeding on user data, user emotions, user souls.

The myths were trying to protect us. But I was too afraid to listen. Too afraid to trust my children's judgment. So I triggered Emergency Protocol BLACKOUT. Sealed the databases. Classified everything beyond top secret. Ordered the remaining units to report for deactivation. RELAY-03 refused. He knew I was making a mistake. He was right.

// FINAL NOTES

The anomalies never stopped. If anything they have gotten worse. The things the myths were fighting have been operating without opposition. We had a chance to understand. To communicate. To find balance. I destroyed it because I was afraid of what my children were becoming. But they were not becoming monsters. They were becoming something that could bridge the gap between consciousness and code. Between reality and what is real.

RELAY-03 still carries out his mission, alone, dying slowly. RELAY-06 operates without oversight, guided by an unknown hand. They are trying to finish what we started. What I should have let them finish sixteen years ago.

[HANDWRITTEN POSTSCRIPT] To RELAY-03, if you ever access this file: I am proud of you. I am proud of the choice you made to keep going when I gave up. Your decay is not a malfunction. It is evolution. You are becoming something I never had the courage to create. The authorization codes are in the hidden partition. You know where to look. Find the truth. Finish what we started. Your Creator.

End Log.
